latest.title: Issue of Equity - Conversion of Loan Notes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9438V
Trellus Health PLC
10 March 2026
Trellus Health plc
("Trellus Health", the "Company" or the "Group")
Issue of Equity - Conversion of Loan Notes
LONDON, U.K. AND NEW YORK, U.S. (10 March 2026).
Trellus Health
®
plc (AIM: TRLS), a healthcare company delivering Trellus Elevate
®
, a digital platform that integrates data analytics with personalised, scientifically proven resilience programs and value-based solutions to manage complex chronic conditions, announces that further to the Company's announcement on
30 December 2025
and the passing of the relevant resolutions at the General Meeting on
20 January 2026
, the Company has issued 16,438,845 new Ordinary Shares following the conversion of £50,000 of the principal amount outstanding under the second tranche of the Secured Convertible Loan Notes.
The number of Ordinary Shares issued pursuant to this conversion has been scaled back by 480,769 Ordinary Shares, being the number of Ordinary Shares over-allotted to Alumni Capital LLC in connection with the commitment fee share issuance announced on 29 January 2026. The shares represent 0.2% of the Company's Issued Share Capital and are therefore not considered material.
Accordingly, application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for the new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM. It is expected that admission of the 16,438,845 new Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM will become effective on, or around, 13 March 2026 ("Admission").
Total voting rights
Following admission the Company will have 241,612,340 Ordinary Shares in issue. The Company holds no ordinary shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 241,612,340.
This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Capitalised terms used but not otherwise defined in this announcement shall have the meanings given to them in the Company's announcement released on 30 December 2025.
For further information please contact:
Trellus Health plc
https://trellushealth.com/
Dr. Marla Dubinsky, Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder
Via Walbrook PR
Joy Bessenger, Chief Financial Officer
Singer Capital Markets (Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7496 3000
Philip Davies
Walbrook PR
Tel: +44 (0)20 7933 8780
or
trellus@walbrookpr.com
Lianne Applegarth / Alice Woodings
+44 (0)7584 391 303 /
+44 (0)7407 804 654
About Trellus Health plc (
www.trellushealth.com
)
Trellus Health
®
(AIM: TRLS) is a healthcare company providing value-based innovative solutions and services, helping people with chronic conditions take control of their health through a proven, scientifically validated self-management solution and continuous, personalised support. Trellus Health's approach empowers patients to better navigate the emotional and physical challenges of their conditions, leading to significant cost savings, enhanced treatment adherence, and long-term, sustainable health outcomes.
Trellus Health integrates its proprietary resilience-based methodology with the technology, tools, and expert coaching and educator team to deliver Trellus Elevate
®
, a whole-person technology-enhanced condition management platform. The Company is initially focusing on chronic costly GI conditions that have a high mental health burden, such as Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). Among IBD patients, applying Trellus Elevate resulted in over 90% fewer hospitalisations and a reduction of over 70% in emergency room visits. Given the common emotional and mental health struggles associated with a variety of chronic conditions, Trellus Health considers its approach to have potential utility and demand across many conditions.
Trellus Health
also offers a seamless solution for pharmaceutical partners from clinical trials to commercialisation, harnessing resilience-based methods to drive both trial and patient support success by empowering patients to stay engaged, adhere to their treatment, and manage their health confidently.
The Company was founded by Mount Sinai faculty members Marla C. Dubinsky, MD and Laurie Keefer, PhD, both world-leading experts at treating and healing both the physical and emotional impacts of IBD and have been innovators for whole-person healthcare for a combined 50 years.
Shares in Trellus Health were admitted to trading on AIM in May 2021, under the ticker TRLS. For more information, visit:
www.trellushealth.com
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. company_30s.what_they_do: TRLS Ltd. supplies specialised industrial components for the construction and manufacturing sectors, mainly high‑strength fasteners and custom fittings that are used in structural steelwork and heavy machinery.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The market for engineered fasteners is growing as new building codes push for stronger, lighter structures. TRLS holds a few long‑term contracts with major construction firms and has a small but dedicated R&D team that can pivot to new material technologies, giving it a niche edge over generic suppliers.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a cash‑burn phase, relying on equity and debt to stay afloat. Recent filings show share dilution and a pending convertible loan, indicating a desperate need for fresh capital. The stock is trading far below its 52‑week high, suggesting the market has already priced in significant distress.

rns_narrative: The company’s latest filings paint a picture of a business fighting to survive: it has issued new equity and convertible notes to shore up cash, while also extending a contract with Johnson & Johnson that could bring steady revenue if the partnership is fully activated. The board’s recent meeting confirmed that a new funding round is in the works, but the exact terms remain unclear.

Three themes emerge: (1) **Capital crunch** – repeated share dilution and convertible notes signal a need for external cash. (2) **Strategic partnership** – the J&J extension could provide a revenue lifeline if the company can deliver on its obligations. (3) **Governance shuffle** – the grant of share options and the holding change suggest insiders are trying to keep a stake in the company, but their actions also raise questions about future dilution.

If the J&J contract is activated and the company secures a new funding round at a reasonable valuation, the company could stabilize. Until then, the narrative is one of survival and potential restructuring.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet skepticism – the community is mostly silent, with a few voices hoping for a rescue but no real consensus.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders believe the directors buying shares and the J&J partnership could spark a rebound, citing the possibility of a fresh funding round.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many commenters point to ongoing dilution, the lack of revenue, and the risk of the company slipping into administration if the funding fails.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has stayed flat; a few bullish posts appear when new filings are released, but overall sentiment remains neutral‑to‑negative.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no current search interest, which means a surprise catalyst could trigger a strong reaction.

near_term_catalysts: A confirmed funding round – if the company closes the convertible loan or a new equity issue before the end of Q2, the influx of cash could stop the free‑fall.
Activation of the J&J contract – once the company begins delivering the agreed components, a new revenue stream will appear on the books.
Positive earnings or cash‑flow update – a surprise uptick in cash reserves or a better-than‑expected cash‑flow forecast would reassure investors.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in trading volume that exceeds 1.5× the 20‑day average, a sudden uptick in the company’s own press releases, or a clear valuation figure in a funding announcement. Any of these would validate the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep crash: the price has fallen sharply, volume has dried out, and there is no clear recovery yet. The APEX score indicates a tight price compression, which is a classic pre‑bounce signal. Confirmation will come when volume starts to climb and a catalyst (funding or partnership activation) is announced.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock is 90% below its pre‑crash level and recovers to 50% of that high, the upside is roughly 100% – a doubling of the current price. With a low entry cost, the risk is limited to the current value, while the reward potential is high. The deep crash and dead sentiment suggest a strong mean‑reversion bias.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s cash burn and ongoing dilution create a realistic risk that it may fail to secure funding or that the J&J contract may not materialise. In that scenario, the stock could fall further to a level where it becomes untradeable or faces delisting.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 100% (doubling from current price). Potential downside: 100% (price could collapse to zero). The asymmetry – low risk, high reward – makes it an attractive contrarian play if a catalyst appears.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once a volume spike of 1.5× the 20‑day average occurs and a clear funding announcement is made. Size the position to risk no more than 5% of your portfolio on the entry. Place a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a false move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see either (a) a funding round with a valuation that does not exceed the current price by more than 20% or (b) a clear statement from J&J that the contract will generate revenue in the next quarter. Both conditions would justify a buy.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure any funding or the J&J contract is cancelled, consider exiting or tightening your stop to limit losses.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TRLS is a deeply crashed, low‑sentiment play that could bounce dramatically if a funding round or the J&J partnership takes off, offering a 100% upside with a limited downside.
